commit.message:
fix(ios): pass PROVISIONING_PROFILE_SPECIFIER + CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY to archive (#1216)

xcodebuild uses these during BUILD (not just export). Without them, it
picks whatever signing config Expo prebuild put in the pbxproj (which
doesn't know about match profiles), failing with "requires a
provisioning profile with Associated Domains, Push Notifications, and
Sign in with Apple features" — the DEFAULT profile Xcode chose lacked
those capabilities, not our match profile.

export_options.provisioningProfiles only applies at export time.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Experiment

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(ios): revert to manual signing — match profiles now have all entitlements (#1215)

Earlier force-regen of match profiles (aa02b594 admin, 02f76c7a
storefront) DID include aps-environment, associated-domains, and
applesignin entitlements. Verified by decrypting the pushed profiles.

The automatic-signing path from #1213 hit an authentication dead-end
(Xcode needs an Apple ID or the ASC API key passed to build_app —
tried both, unstable).

Manual signing with match is the canonical, reproducible pattern for
Expo + Fastlane on GitHub Actions. Now works because:
1. App IDs have all required capabilities registered
2. Match profiles regenerated with those capabilities
3. DEVELOPMENT_TEAM passed via xcargs so xcodebuild knows the team

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Archive Pitch Detective prototype

Reconstruct the public repo from the local audited build artifacts.

The raw audit.jsonl is intentionally excluded because it contains the full agent transcript and sensitive execution metadata. The repo keeps the runnable single-file app, the gist deployment helper, and documentation describing the detection pipeline and provenance.

Attribution: the original prototype captured in the audit log was generated in a Claude Sonnet 4.6 session, with a later pitch-detection review by Claude Haiku 4.5. Repository packaging and documentation in this commit were done with OpenAI Codex.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(ios): switch to automatic signing with allowProvisioningUpdates (#1213)

Match-generated profiles were missing Associated Domains, Push
Notifications, and Sign in with Apple entitlements. Apple's API doesn't
auto-register capabilities when regenerating profiles — only Xcode with
-allowProvisioningUpdates does, which is why local archives work.

Switch build_app to automatic signing + -allowProvisioningUpdates. Xcode
now fetches the ASC API key (provided by app_store_connect_api_key in
before_all), auto-registers capabilities on the App ID, and issues a
profile that includes them.

Match still runs before build_app to install the Apple Distribution cert
in the keychain (so Xcode reuses it instead of hitting the 2-cert limit).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(deps): dedupe @types/react to single 19.2.14 (#1208)

pnpm was hoisting 19.2.13 at the root while nested copies resolved to
19.2.14 (via react-native, zustand, expo-router, testing-library, etc.),
causing TS2769 "two different types with this name exist" errors on
react-native ref props (e.g. TextInput ref in AuthInput.tsx).

Pinning to 19.2.14 via pnpm.overrides collapses all 8 copies to one.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ios): let setup_ci manage keychain, don't override name (#1211)

MATCH_KEYCHAIN_NAME was pointing match at a keychain that setup_ci never
creates — it creates fastlane_tmp_keychain internally. Removing the
override lets setup_ci + match coordinate on the same keychain.

Fixes iOS Production Release failure:
"Could not locate the provided keychain"

commit.message:
Implement plural rules in ars-i18n (#530)

* Implement plural rules in ars-i18n

Add ICU4X and web-intl plural backends, coverage integration, and spec sync for issues #126 and #131.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Restrict web-intl plural backend to wasm

Keep non-wasm web-intl builds on the documented English fallback path and add a regression test for that behavior.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Align fallback plural rules for negative one

Normalize fallback cardinal selection to treat -1 like 1 and cover the shared fallback path with a regression test.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Handle non-finite plural inputs consistently

Return Other for non-finite ICU plural inputs and document the cross-backend behavior in the i18n spec.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Make wasm plural backends mutually exclusive

Ensure the wasm web-intl plural backend is cfg-exclusive from icu4x so combined feature builds fail through the intended crate-level compile_error instead of duplicate definitions.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(ios): cap ASC API key duration at 1200s (Apple max) (#1210)

Apple rejects any duration > 1200s (20 min) on app_store_connect_api_key.
The 1800s bump from the previous review nit was invalid and broke CI.

Fixes iOS Production Release workflow failure on run 24359652829:
"The duration can't be more than 1200 (20 minutes) and the value entered was '1800'"

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Sideloading: App can trigger jobs that will not be sent to ODS (#45)

* make job.ods_id nullable, add job.send_to_ods column

Migration 030: drops NOT NULL on ods_id, adds send_to_ods boolean
(default true), adds CHECK constraint enforcing ods_id IS NOT NULL
OR send_to_ods = false. All existing rows unaffected.

Fixes downstream TS errors from the now-optional odsConfig Prisma
relation and adds sendToOds + rosterFilePath fields to the executor
DTO (behavioral wiring comes in a later commit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* finish no-ods job submission flow

resolve school-year config server-side so jobs can skip ODS safely while the executor gets the roster path and credentials it needs for no-ODS runs

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* address PR 5 review findings

- Fix type safety for STS roster resource: update ExecutorService interface
  to require `Run & { job: Job & { schoolYear: SchoolYear } }`, removing the
  unsafe cast in executor.aws.service.ts. The compiler now enforces that the
  Prisma include is present at the call site.
- Add external API integration test for the "no config row" case, verifying
  a 400 with "School year is not enabled" when no school_year_config row
  exists.
- Switch tenant config query in JobCreatePage from useQuery to
  useSuspenseQuery for consistency with the job templates query; remove the
  redundant null guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* condition assessmentDatastore on sendToOds, move rosterFilePath inline

Review round 2 fixes: assessmentDatastore is now gated on
job.sendToOds (matching rosterFilePath and the kickoff spec)
instead of odsConfig?.activeConnection. Roster path computation
moved inline to only evaluate for no-ODS jobs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add test: reject no-ODS jobs when roster file is missing

Red commit — test expects 400 with "No roster file found" when
a no-ODS job is submitted and doFilesExist returns false.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* validate roster file exists for no-ODS jobs, use configurable roster bucket

Adds rosterBucket() to AppConfigService (defaults to S3_FILE_UPLOAD_BUCKET,
independently configurable). rosterFilePath in the executor payload is now
a fully qualified S3 URI with the roster filename hardcoded to
studentEducationOrganizationAssociations.jsonl.

resolveJobDestination checks S3 for the roster file before allowing
no-ODS job creation, returning roster_file_missing if absent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add tests: hasRoster on tenant school year config endpoint

Red commit — tests expect hasRoster boolean per year, returning
false when doFilesExist mock returns false.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* return hasRoster on tenant config endpoint, disable years without roster

Tenant school year config endpoint now checks S3 for the roster file
per year and returns hasRoster. Frontend disables no-ODS years that
lack a roster with "(no roster file loaded)" label.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix pr 5 review follow-ups

Align the no-ODS payload test with the documented full roster S3 URI and set a concrete test bucket so the payload cannot degrade to s3://undefined. Also surface roster_file_missing distinctly in the external API and cover that branch in integration tests so clients get the right remediation path.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* tighten tenant roster availability contract

return hasRoster as null for send-to-ODS school years so the API distinguishes not relevant from missing. only probe S3 for no-ODS years, and only enable no-ODS year selection in the UI when hasRoster is explicitly true.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix roster S3 error handling and bucket selection

doFilesExist was catching all S3 errors and returning false, masking
AWS outages/permission errors as "no roster file found". Now only
NotFound (404) returns false; other errors rethrow as operational
failures. Roster checks now use appConfig.rosterBucket() instead of
s3Bucket(), honoring the independently-configurable bucket decision.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update seedJob to support no-ODS jobs, tighten return type

seedJob now accepts sendToOds: false with a schoolYearId instead of
requiring odsConfig. Return type narrowed to SeededJob so callers
know runs (and nested relations) are always present — removes the
need for runtime guards. Existing callers unchanged (sendToOds
defaults to true, odsConfig still required in that branch).

Also migrates the manual prisma.job.create in earthbeam-api.spec.ts
to use the updated factory, and removes the now-unnecessary runs
existence guards throughout the file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add tests: no-ODS read path, hasRoster happy path, S3 error propagation

Covers three review gaps:
- GET /jobs and GET /jobs/:id now verify odsId=null and sendToOds=false
  for no-ODS jobs (guards the read/view regression area)
- GET /school-year-config/tenant asserts hasRoster=true when a roster
  file exists for a no-ODS year (the UI-enabling happy path)
- POST /jobs verifies that a non-404 S3 error from doFilesExist
  propagates as 500 instead of being swallowed as "roster missing"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* polish seedJob and makePostJobDto signatures

seedJob now uses a discriminated union with `never` guards so each
branch has exactly one way to provide schoolYearId — callers can't
construct a plausible-looking but inconsistent set of args. The
narrowing ternary keys off sendToOds and produces odsId, schoolYearId,
and sendToOds in one shot.

makePostJobDto takes a plain schoolYearId string instead of an
OdsConfig, since that's the only field it ever used.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* consolidate tenant config tests, seed a sendToOds=false year

Flip sycA2526 to sendToOds=false so partner A's seed data covers both
configurations. Collapse the six single-aspect GET /tenant happy-path
tests into one toMatchObject assertion that verifies both row shapes
(send-to-ODS and no-ODS) in a single request. Remove the standalone
hasRoster=true test (absorbed into the happy path) and simplify the
hasRoster=false test to use the seeded config directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* extract rosterFileKey helper, tighten STS roster policy

Deduplicate the roster S3 key construction (4 call sites) into
earthbeam/roster-path.ts. The helper takes structural { partnerId,
tenantCode } and { endYear } params so call sites pass domain objects
directly. Also tightens the STS policy from a wildcard prefix to the
exact roster file, and replaces the ! assertion on the tenant config
query result with an explicit guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* clean up executor payload: remove ODS assertions, add sendToOds to slack event

Replace ! assertions on odsConfig/activeConnection with an early
odsConnection extraction that TS can narrow naturally. Add sendToOds
flag to the run_complete event payload so Slack notifications can
distinguish no-ODS runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* make doFilesExist bucket param required, tighten bucket return types

Every caller passes a bucket explicitly, so drop the default. Narrow
s3Bucket() and rosterBucket() return types to string since the env var
is required infrastructure config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* remove odsId from job DTOs

odsId is an internal implementation detail — the frontend doesn't use
it and the external API never exposed it. Remove from IBaseJobDto,
GetJobDto, and clean up the now-unnecessary omit on PostJobDto.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* remove odsId from job DTOs, flatten tenant query export

Drop odsId from IBaseJobDto and GetJobDto — it's an internal detail
unused by the frontend or external API. Split tenantSchoolYearConfigQuery
into its own top-level export and rename both query configs from plural
to singular to reflect that each is a single query config object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* remove multiple_ods_found error from job destination resolution

The unique index uq_ods_config_tenant_partner_year_active (migration
025) prevents duplicate ODS configs per tenant/partner/year, making
this check dead code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* move error messages from service to controllers

resolveJobDestination now returns only { status, code } for errors.
Each controller constructs user-facing messages from the code using
an exhaustive Record keyed by the error code union, so new codes
added to the service will cause a TS error if not handled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* extract doesFileExist for single-file S3 checks

Factor out the single-file HeadObject logic from doFilesExist into
doesFileExist, and rewrite doFilesExist to delegate to it. Callers
that were wrapping a single key in an array now call doesFileExist
directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* remove odsId assertions from job integration tests

odsId was removed from GetJobDto in this PR, so the serializer strips
it from responses. The typed assertion (line 100) caused a TS compile
failure that prevented the entire test file from running. The untyped
res.body assertions were unreachable for the same reason.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add comment explaining single-ODS assumption in resolveJobDestination

The [0] access on the odsConfig array is safe due to a DB uniqueness
constraint, but this is a common source of confusion for reviewers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* improve local dev setup: s3mock, roster seeding, roles, year configs

- pin s3mock to v4.12.4 (was `latest`, which pulled a day-one breaking
  change in v5.0.0 that removed legacy property names); fix env var
  names to the full spring boot relaxed binding form
  (COM_ADOBE_TESTING_S3MOCK_STORE_*) and enable file retention so the
  named volume is actually used
- add app/api/local-data/ with a synthetic roster file and seed it into
  s3mock for all three school years during init.sh
- seed school_year_config rows for partner ea: 2324 and 2425 as no-ods
  years, 2526 as an ods year
- add keycloak realm roles (User, PartnerAdmin), assign User to dev and
  PartnerAdmin to admin, and add a roles protocol mapper so the roles
  claim is present in the jwt
- update oidc_config seed to set roles_claim = 'roles'; use ON CONFLICT
  DO UPDATE so existing dev environments pick it up on re-seed
- make bundle sync non-fatal in init.sh with a clear message for vpn
  environments that block raw.githubusercontent.com
- add /.scratch to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update axios to 1.15.0 to resolve GHSA-3p68-rc4w-qgx5

Fixes the critical-rated NO_PROXY hostname normalization bypass (SSRF).
Runway is not practically affected given the AWS deployment has no proxy
configured, but bumping to the fix release as a precaution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* address review round 6: redundant filter, seedJob cleanup, type comment

Remove redundant isEnabled filter from tenant config include (already
guaranteed by the top-level some + PK constraint), drop seedJob's
explicit return type in favor of Prisma inference, flip the sendToOds
ternary to avoid a double-negative, and add a type-narrowing comment
in JobCreatePage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: runway
repository.description: Webapp that loads assessment results to an Ed-Fi ODS

commit.message:
fix(web): restore static root app shell (#1199)

* fix(web): restore static root app shell

* fix(auth): add loading.tsx to provide implicit Suspense for signup

After removing the root Suspense boundary, /signup's `await cookies()`
needed its own boundary. Adding loading.tsx provides Next.js's implicit
Suspense wrapper for the route segment, allowing the static prerender
to complete without a HANGING_PROMISE_REJECTION on the cookie read.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): add loading.tsx for /[slug]/[category] Suspense

* fix(prerender): wrap dynamic page bodies in Suspense for Cache Components

Next.js 16 Cache Components mode requires uncached data access
(await cookies(), await params, await searchParams) to be inside
a <Suspense> boundary. loading.tsx provides implicit Suspense for
the segment but not for the page component's own top-level awaits.

Split signup and [slug]/[category] pages into a thin Suspense wrapper
and an inner async content component so the awaits run inside the
boundary, unblocking the static prerender.

Replaces the earlier loading.tsx attempt (insufficient for the
HANGING_PROMISE_REJECTION / Uncached-data errors).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): replace blank Suspense fallbacks with visible loaders

- Signup: inline spinner with role="status"/aria-busy so users see
  progress while cookies() + supabase.auth.getUser() resolve
- Category: reuse ProductGridSkeleton (already imported) so users
  see a skeleton grid instead of a blank screen

Addresses CodeRabbit concerns about fallback={null} causing blank
screens during the Suspense window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(web): harden category page route assertions

* fix(1199): address CodeRabbit review

- Signup: restore top-level auth check + redirect(). Add
  `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` so the uncached cookies()
  read and the redirect happen above any Suspense boundary, per
  CodeRabbit's concern about redirect-under-Suspense control flow.
  Signup is per-request anyway (reads session cookie), so static
  generation was not applicable.

- Category test: export CategoryPageContent so tests can invoke it
  directly. Update the notFound and passthrough tests to call
  CategoryPageContent (the async inner) instead of the now-sync
  CategoryPageRoute wrapper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): remove unused CategoryPageRoute import

* fix(signup): revert to Suspense wrap (Cache Components disallows force-dynamic)

Next.js 16 Cache Components mode rejects `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'`
with the error: "Route segment config 'dynamic' is not compatible with
nextConfig.cacheComponents". The only way to satisfy the "uncached data outside
Suspense" prerender guard is to wrap the request-scoped work in a Suspense
boundary.

redirect() still works correctly inside the Suspense boundary because it
throws a NEXT_REDIRECT error (not a thenable/promise), which bubbles past
React's Suspense handler and is caught by Next.js' server-side redirect
mechanism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): add local suspense boundary to slug layout

* fix(test): correct storefront layout mock typing

* fix(blog): move headers under page suspense

* fix(blog): add local suspense to platform post page

* fix(storefront): add local suspense to entry routes

* fix(build): isolate builder prerender blockers

* fix(app): add local suspense to cookie routes

* fix(admin): add local suspense boundary

* fix(invite): add local suspense boundary

* fix(app): restore static root suspense boundary

commit.message:
feat: add iOS production release pipeline (parity with Android) (#1206)

* feat: add iOS production release pipeline (parity with Android)

Mirrors android-release.yml / android-storefront-release.yml: push to main →
GitHub Actions builds signed IPA via Fastlane → uploads to App Store Connect
→ submits for review → auto-releases on Apple approval. Reduces iOS releases
to a single human step (Apple's review).

- ios-release.yml + ios-storefront-release.yml (macos-14, Xcode 16.2)
- Fastlane release lane per app (build_app + match + upload_to_app_store)
- Code signing via fastlane match (encrypted certs in private repo)
- IOS_BUILD_NUMBER env support in app.config.ts mirrors ANDROID_VERSION_CODE
- Storefront ascAppId set (was placeholder)

Required GitHub secrets: ASC_API_KEY_ID, ASC_API_ISSUER_ID,
ASC_API_KEY_P8_BASE64, MATCH_GIT_URL, MATCH_PASSWORD, MATCH_DEPLOY_KEY.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dependency Review blocked highline, httpclient, json, concurrent-ruby
which use the Ruby license (permissive, similar to BSD).

commit.message:
fix(release): publish 3.6.7 stdio shutdown fix

Summary:
- make uru-mcp exit cleanly when Claude closes stdio or a broken
  pipe triggers EPIPE
- keep dynamic tool refresh enabled by default while preserving
  @latest launcher guidance for Claude clients
- sync package metadata, docs, verify snapshot, tests, and tarball
  for the 3.6.7 release

Rationale:
- orphaned stdio server processes were lingering after client
  disconnects and later crashing during tool-sync writes
- those stale processes compounded npx relaunch churn and led to the
  ENOTEMPTY cache failures users were seeing on reconnect

Tests:
- npm test
- npm pack
- manual local repro comparing published @latest hanging on closed
  stdin vs the patched local binary exiting cleanly

feat(tracemetrics): Add column sorting to samples table (#112577)

Add click-to-sort on the metrics samples table for `value` and
`timestamp` columns, bringing it to parity with the aggregates table
which already had sorting.

Sort state is persisted in the URL so it survives page reloads. The
samples sort and aggregate sort are stored independently — a new
`aggregateOrderby` field is added to saved queries so each table retains
its own sort order when saving and restoring queries.

Sort field names are validated against a shared
`SORTABLE_SAMPLE_COLUMNS` set on decode, falling back to the default
(`timestamp desc`) for unknown or malformed values. Backwards
compatibility is maintained: saved queries without `aggregateOrderby`
fall back to deriving the aggregate sort from `orderby` as before.

Closes LOGS-633

commit.message:
fix(slack): Preserve team id for edited mention streaming (#182)

Carry Slack's top-level `team_id` through synthesized `message_changed`
mention payloads so native streaming replies can derive
`recipient_team_id` when an edited message newly mentions Junior.

The failure in production was not in normal mention routing or model
generation; it happened when the edited-mention side path built a
synthetic message without the team metadata that Chat SDK's native Slack
streaming requires for `chat.startStream`. That let the turn reach the
first streamed reply post and then fail with a validation error.

This also extends the shared Slack MSW harness for `chat.startStream`,
`chat.appendStream`, and `chat.stopStream`, then adds an integration
regression that drives a signed `message_changed` webhook through the
real ingress path and asserts the native stream request includes both
recipient identifiers.

I considered covering this only with a unit assertion on the synthesized
message shape, but the production gap was specifically the combination
of webhook ingress plus native streaming, so the regression belongs at
that integration seam.

Fixes JUNIOR-1K

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Keep first-party routing and virtual scrolling aligned with real browsers

This commit finalizes two isolated runtime fixes left in the working tree.
The proxy now recognizes same-origin requests by comparing the browser's
Origin against the Host header, which keeps first-party API access working
when Next.js dev normalizes origins oddly on LAN or local hostnames. The
shared virtual list also mirrors its self-scroll element into a ref at
commit time so TanStack Virtual subscribes reliably instead of rendering 0
items when React callback refs schedule state too late.

Constraint: Browser-origin checks must stay strict for cross-origin traffic while still allowing real first-party requests in local and LAN development
Constraint: TanStack Virtual subscribes to the scroll element synchronously during mount, so callback-ref state alone can miss the first subscription window
Rejected: Loosen proxy origin checks globally | would weaken same-origin guarantees for protected API routes
Rejected: Keep virtual list on state-only callback refs | intermittent empty virtualization state remains possible on first mount
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Preserve the Host-header fallback and commit-time scroll ref mirror unless a replacement is verified against local, LAN, and restricted-browser contexts
Tested: pnpm test tests/list-virtualization.test.ts tests/salesforce-feed-auth.test.ts
Not-tested: Full manual QA across every first-party route that depends on proxy same-origin detection
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix TH2 image and zoom redraw behavior

Make the TH2 image layer stay mounted so image-specific redraws are handled
inside MPImagesWidget instead of forcing broader canvas rebuilds. This fixes
newly inserted JPEGs not appearing until an unrelated redraw while keeping the
image visibility logic local to the image widget.

Also keep zoom-to-selection inert with empty selection, preserve the
selection-window zoom fixes for plain clicks and tiny drags, and extend widget
coverage for the new-file plus inserted-JPEG flows.

commit.message:
Keep automation and scroll helpers from fighting runtime reality

This commit hardens the remaining operational paths that were still open
in the working tree: authenticated scrape triggers can bypass the public
IP rate limiter, the shared virtual list now holds its self-scroll node in
state, Flextender drops a stray form field, and the embedding batch now
uses the final embedding text as its eligibility gate. It also teaches the
scraper health task to force-reset platforms that have been stuck behind a
circuit breaker for 48 hours and routes Trigger Sentry init through the
same safe DSN validation used elsewhere.

Constraint: Automation endpoints need to stay usable for cron and internal callers without weakening the public rate limit path
Constraint: Embedding backfills must remain cheap and deterministic on the visible vacature set
Rejected: Keep relying on raw sourceText checks before embedding | duplicated logic and diverged from the actual text sent to embeddings
Rejected: Leave stale scraper circuit breakers untouched indefinitely | keeps known-bad platforms wedged without recovery pressure
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: If scrape-start auth changes again, preserve the split between bearer-authorized automation and public IP-throttled access
Tested: pnpm test tests/embeddings-batch.test.ts tests/list-virtualization.test.ts tests/flextender-hours.test.ts tests/trigger-config-env-sync.test.ts
Not-tested: End-to-end browser interaction for the scrape start button and long-running stale breaker recovery in production time
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Reduce skills complexity and keep scraper + Sentry stable in runtime

This completes the current LinkedIn-style skills cleanup by routing the
remaining app, AI, MCP, and voice surfaces through the simplified
canonical skill model and removing the root app dependency on the
workspace ESCO package. It also hardens scraper runtime behavior with
short-lived caches and request-level fallbacks so the dashboard keeps
rendering during Neon timeouts, and it adds defensive Sentry DSN
validation so malformed DSNs no longer produce browser console noise.

Constraint: The app still needs compatibility aliases because several public and internal surfaces still speak in canonicalSkills and legacy ESCO-shaped payloads during migration
Constraint: Scraper dashboard reliability had to improve without rewriting the entire data model or depending on a stable external database during dev
Rejected: Full removal of the esco facade and payload field names in one pass | too risky while downstream consumers still expect compatibility
Rejected: Let invalid Sentry DSNs flow into SDK init and rely on runtime warnings | noisy and user-visible in development
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: broad
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the compatibility shims until the remaining escoUri/ESCO naming debt and legacy endpoints are intentionally retired in a follow-up cleanup
Tested: pnpm lint; pnpm exec tsc --noEmit; pnpm test; zsh -lc 'set -a; source /tmp/motian.env; set +a; pnpm build'; runtime checks for /, /kandidaten, /vacatures, /chat, /scraper, /api/gezondheid on dev server
Not-tested: Authenticated /api/vaardigheden behavior and correcting the underlying Sentry DSN value in env storage
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- "Introducing Claude Managed Agents" (anthropic.com/news/claude-managed-agents)
  HTTP 404 — URL does not exist on Anthropic's site.
- "Claude Cowork is now generally available" (anthropic.com/news/claude-cowork-ga)
  HTTP 404 — URL does not exist.
- "Introducing upgrades to Codex" (openai.com/index/introducing-upgrades-to-codex/)
  Dated 2026-04-11 but absent from the live OpenAI RSS top-30 despite being
  recent enough to appear; very likely fabricated by WebSearch fallback.

These entries slipped in while trigger-side WebFetch was 403-blocked and the
prompt was allowed to synthesize from WebSearch snippets. The new architecture
(local fetcher → cache JSON → trigger reads cache, no fallback) prevents this
class of error going forward. Prior daily/weekly report files are left as
historical record; future reports will no longer reference these entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
